# Modular Pipelines Documentation

> Documentation for ModularPipelines - a C# framework for building modular, testable CI/CD pipelines with dependency injection support.

This file contains links to documentation sections following the llmstxt.org standard.

## Table of Contents

- [Modular Pipelines](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/README): Modular Pipelines is a framework for writing your pipeline in C#. Giving you strong types, intellisense, parallelisation, and the entire .NET ecosy...
- [Module Execution Lifecycle](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/architecture/module-execution-lifecycle): This document describes the complete execution lifecycle of a module in ModularPipelines, including the order of operations for hooks, skip checks,...
- [Architecture](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/architecture): This page describes the internal architecture of distributed mode for contributors and advanced users.
- [Capabilities and Routing](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/capabilities): Not every worker can execute every module. Some modules need Docker, others need a specific OS. The capability system controls how modules are rout...
- [Configuration](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/configuration): Distributed mode has two layers of configuration: the core `DistributedOptions` (shared across all coordinator implementations) and coordinator-spe...
- [Getting Started](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/getting-started): This guide walks you through adding distributed execution to an existing ModularPipelines project using Redis as the coordinator.
- [CI Example: GitHub Actions](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/github-actions): This is a complete example of running a distributed pipeline across GitHub Actions matrix runners using Redis for coordination.
- [Overview](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/distributed/overview): Distributed mode lets you split a ModularPipelines pipeline across multiple processes or machines. Instead of running every module in a single proc...
- [Azure Resource Provisioning Example](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/examples/azure-example): So for example, we want to provision some Azure services like this:
- [.NET Test, Build & Publish](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/examples/dotnet-test-build-publish): Here's an example of publishing a NuGet package. Complete with generating sematic versioning, and running tests.
- [Fully Orchestrated Example](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/examples/example): The pipeline to test, generate and upload the NuGet packages for this library.. is made from this library.
- [F# Interactive Example](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/examples/fsharp-interactive): F# Interactive (FSI) is a powerful tool for executing F# code snippets and scripts in an interactive environment. It allows you to quickly test and...
- [Single File C# Example](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/examples/single-file-csharp): Starting with dotnet 10 (preview 4), you can use the new file-based C# application feature to write scripts in C# without needing to create a full ...
- [Fundamentals](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/fundamentals): Your pipeline is created using `Pipeline.CreateBuilder()`. This follows the ASP.NET Core minimal API pattern, providing direct access to `Configura...
- [Always Run](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/always-run): By default, modules only run if their dependencies succeed. If a dependency fails, the module waiting will not start and will abort.
- [Analyzers](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/analyzers): These include:
- [Cancellation Tokens](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/cancellation-tokens): When you override a Module's `ExecuteAsync` method, you are provided a `CancellationToken` by the framework.
- [Categories](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/categories): ```csharp
- [Console Progress](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/console-progress): If you are using an interactive terminal, then a progress dialog will be displayed to you. This will attempt to show you estimated remaining time, ...
- [Custom Commands](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/custom-commands): If you want to run a command that isn't currently supported by strong objects, you can still run commands directly through the `ICommand` interface...
- [Defining Modules](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/defining-modules): Modules are defined by creating a class that inherits from the `Module<T>` base class.
- [Execution and Dependencies](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/execution-and-dependencies): The default behaviour is for modules to run in parallel, to speed up a pipeline as much as possible.
- [Hooks](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/hooks): There are three ways to hook into module execution:
- [Ignoring Failures](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/ignoring-failures): Sometimes a module might throw an exception, but we simply don't care as it's not that important, or a specific error might be expected.
- [Inheriting](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/inheriting): Each 'Module' is expected to be registered only once. If you build a custom module that you'd like to instantiate multiple times but with different...
- [Logging](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/logging): This is because the `IModuleLogger` will perform many actions that will improve the output of your logs.
- [Parallelization](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/parallelization): As mentioned, modules will try to run in parallel by default, waiting on any dependencies if they need to.
- [Pipeline Host](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/pipeline-host): To begin creating your pipeline, use the `Pipeline.CreateBuilder()` method.
- [Pipeline Execution Modes](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/pipeline-modes): A pipeline has two execution modes:
- [Requirements](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/requirements): If you'd like to fail fast, you can register some `Requirement` classes that do some checks on start up to make sure things are as expected.
- [Retry Policies](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/retry-policy): When creating modules, you can set a retry policy per module using the `Configure()` method. The retry policy uses Polly, so if you've used Polly b...
- [Run conditions](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/run-conditions): Run conditions are generally controlled by attributes, placed upon your module.
- [Secrets](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/secrets): Your options classes should be registered as IOptions.
- [Sharing data across modules](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/sharing-data): Modules have been designed with data and sharing at its core.
- [Skipping Modules](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/skipping): The recommended way to configure module skipping is through the `Configure()` method with the fluent builder API:
- [Module History](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/storing-and-retrieving-results): This can be used later on if you want to re-run the pipeline but skip certain categories or modules.
- [Sub-Modules](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/sub-modules): For instance, you have 10 .NET projects to package into NuGet packages.
- [Testing](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/testing): ModularPipelines supports mocking file system operations for unit testing. All file I/O goes through `IFileSystemProvider`, which can be replaced w...
- [Time Estimator](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/time-estimator): The time estimator is a class built by you, used to estimate times for modules for displaying in the console progress dialog. It isn't mandatory, b...
- [Timeouts](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/how-to/timeouts): Modules have a 30-minute timeout by default. Configure the pipeline default when your workloads need a different limit, or use `TimeSpan.Zero` to d...
- [Migrating to V3](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/migrating-to-v3): ModularPipelines V3 is a major release that modernizes the API to follow ASP.NET Core minimal API patterns. This guide covers all breaking changes ...
- [Amazon Web Services Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/amazon-web-services): Package reserved for strongly typed AWS CLI commands and AWS SDK helpers.
- [Ansible Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/ansible): `ModularPipelines.Ansible` provides strongly typed access to Ansible's ad-hoc command CLI.
- [Argo CD Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/argocd): `ModularPipelines.ArgoCd` provides strongly typed access to the Argo CD CLI, including
- [Azure Pipelines Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/azure-pipelines): Azure Pipelines environment and build integration helpers.
- [Azure Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/azure): Azure SDK helpers and strongly typed Azure CLI commands.
- [Buildah Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/buildah): Strongly typed Buildah commands for building OCI container images.
- [Chocolatey Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/chocolatey): Strongly typed Chocolatey package-management commands.
- [Command Prompt Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/cmd): Helpers for executing Windows Command Prompt commands.
- [Cosign Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/cosign): `ModularPipelines.Cosign` provides strongly typed access to the Sigstore Cosign CLI for
- [Distributed S3 Artifacts Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/distributed-artifacts-s3): `ModularPipelines.Distributed.Artifacts.S3` stores distributed pipeline artifacts in AWS S3 or an S3-compatible service such as Cloudflare R2, Back...
- [Distributed Redis Discovery Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/distributed-discovery-redis): `ModularPipelines.Distributed.Discovery.Redis` lets distributed SignalR workers discover the current master through Redis.
- [Distributed Redis Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/distributed-redis): `ModularPipelines.Distributed.Redis` provides Redis-backed coordination and artifact storage for distributed pipelines.
- [Distributed SignalR Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/distributed-signalr): `ModularPipelines.Distributed.SignalR` provides SignalR-based coordination between distributed pipeline workers.
- [Docker Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/docker): Strongly typed Docker container, image, network, volume, and Buildx commands.
- [.NET Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/dotnet): Strongly typed .NET CLI commands, builders, and TRX test-result parsing.
- [Eksctl Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/eksctl): `ModularPipelines.Eksctl` provides strongly typed access to the eksctl CLI for
- [Email Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/email): Email delivery helpers for pipeline notifications.
- [Flux Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/flux): Strongly typed Flux CLI commands for GitOps workflows.
- [Flyway Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/flyway): Strongly typed Flyway database migration commands.
- [FTP Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/ftp): FTP file-transfer helpers for pipeline modules.
- [Git Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/git): Git repository information, versioning, and strongly typed Git commands.
- [GitHub Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/github): The **GitHub package** offered by **Modular Pipelines**, in conjunction with the implemented **OctoKit client library**, facilitates a streamlined ...
- [Go Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/go): Strongly typed Go toolchain commands.
- [Google Cloud Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/google): Strongly typed Google Cloud CLI commands.
- [Grype Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/grype): Strongly typed Grype vulnerability-scanning commands.
- [Hadolint Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/hadolint): `ModularPipelines.Hadolint` provides strongly typed access to the Hadolint Dockerfile linter.
- [Helm Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/helm): Strongly typed Helm package-management commands for Kubernetes.
- [Homebrew Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/homebrew): Strongly typed Homebrew package-management commands.
- [Java Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/java): `ModularPipelines.Java` provides strongly typed access to Maven and Gradle build CLIs.
- [jq Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/jq): `ModularPipelines.Jq` provides strongly typed access to the jq JSON processor CLI.
- [kind Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/kind): Strongly typed kind commands for local Kubernetes clusters.
- [Kubernetes Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/kubernetes): Strongly typed kubectl and Kustomize commands.
- [Liquibase Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/liquibase): Package reserved for Liquibase database change-management commands.
- [Microsoft Teams Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/microsoft-teams): Microsoft Teams notification helpers.
- [minikube Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/minikube): Strongly typed minikube commands for local Kubernetes clusters.
- [Newman Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/newman): Strongly typed Newman commands for Postman collections.
- [Node.js Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/node): Node.js, npm, npx, nvm, and strongly typed pnpm helpers.
- [Packer Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/packer): Strongly typed Packer machine-image build commands.
- [Podman Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/podman): Strongly typed Podman container-management commands.
- [Pulumi Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/pulumi): Strongly typed Pulumi infrastructure commands.
- [Python Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/python): Strongly typed pip package-management commands.
- [Rust Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/rust): Strongly typed Cargo commands for Rust projects.
- [ShellCheck Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/shellcheck): The `ModularPipelines.Shellcheck` package provides strongly typed access to the ShellCheck static analysis CLI.
- [Skopeo Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/skopeo): Strongly typed Skopeo container-image commands.
- [Slack Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/slack): Slack notification helpers.
- [Snyk Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/snyk): `ModularPipelines.Snyk` provides strongly typed access to the Snyk CLI for open-source,
- [SonarScanner Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/sonar-scanner): Package reserved for SonarScanner code-analysis commands.
- [Syft Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/syft): Strongly typed Syft software-bill-of-materials commands.
- [TeamCity Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/teamcity): TeamCity environment and build integration helpers.
- [Terraform Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/terraform): Strongly typed Terraform infrastructure commands.
- [Trivy Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/trivy): Package reserved for Trivy vulnerability and misconfiguration scanning commands.
- [Vault Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/vault): Strongly typed HashiCorp Vault commands.
- [WinGet Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/winget): Strongly typed Windows Package Manager commands.
- [Yarn Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/yarn): Package reserved for strongly typed Yarn package-management commands.
- [yq Package](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/mp-packages/yq): Strongly typed yq YAML-processing commands.
- [Why Modular Pipelines?](https://thomhurst.github.io/ModularPipelines/docs/why): - The .NET ecosystem is rich with features and libraries. You can utilize what already exists and not have to re-invent the wheel.
